slides.title: Since
lines: The popularization of the IPv6 protocol solved the addressing problem, and the establishment of LoRa and NB-IoT standards promoted the maturity of LPWAN technology; China issued a national strategy, and the IoT industry entered the initial stage of accelerated development.

The commercialization of 5G and the release of HarmonyOS built a solid technical foundation, and the global pandemic accelerated the implementation of scenarios such as telemedicine and contactless delivery; AIoT became the industry mainstream, and the platform-level competition pattern gradually stabilized.

The Matter protocol has received extensive support to break ecological barriers, and IoT has become increasingly integrated with the metaverse and digital twins; application scenarios have expanded in depth from the consumer end to core fields such as industrial manufacturing and smart cities.
slides.text:
Since
2010
01 Technological Foundation and Early Applications (2010-2018)
The popularization of the IPv6 protocol solved the addressing problem, and the establishment of LoRa and NB-IoT standards promoted the maturity of LPWAN technology; China issued a national strategy, and the IoT industry entered the initial stage of accelerated development.
02 Rapid Development and Ecological Expansion (2019-2023)
The commercialization of 5G and the release of HarmonyOS built a solid technical foundation, and the global pandemic accelerated the implementation of scenarios such as telemedicine and contactless delivery; AIoT became the industry mainstream, and the platform-level competition pattern gradually stabilized.
03 Standard Unification and Deep Integration (2024-2026)
The Matter protocol has received extensive support to break ecological barriers, and IoT has become increasingly integrated with the metaverse and digital twins; application scenarios have expanded in depth from the consumer end to core fields such as industrial manufacturing and smart cities.

Scale and Targets: It successfully infected nearly 10,000 IoT devices in just 5 days, mainly targeting unprotected edge devices such as home and commercial cameras, and routers. The attack purpose is clear: it uses controlled "bots" to launch Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks, causing severe congestion to the target network services.
Case 2: Joint Attack by Aisuru and Kimwolf (2025-2026)

Super Scale and Destruction: A huge botnet composed of 1 to 4 million infected devices set a new record with a DDoS attack traffic peak of over 30 Tbps. In access security, the access security of the sensing layer is the key point. First, a sensing node cannot be accessed by a node or system that has not been authenticated and authorized, which involves the security requirements of the sensing node's trust management, identity authentication, and access control. Therefore, in addition to being subject to the same security threats as existing networks, sensor networks may also be subject to security threats such as attacks from malicious nodes, monitoring or destruction of transmitted data, and poor data consistency.

slides.title: Security Demands of IoT
lines: Due to the exponential growth of communication terminals in the

and the limited carrying capacity of existing communication networks, when a large number of network terminal nodes access the existing network, it will bring more security threats to the communication network.

First, the access of a large number of terminal nodes will definitely bring about network congestion, and network congestion will give attackers an opportunity to take advantage of, thereby causing a denial of service attack on the server;
Second, due to the small amount of data transmitted by devices in the
, complex encryption algorithms are generally not used to protect data, which may cause data to be attacked and destroyed during transmission;
Finally, the integration of the sensing layer and the network layer will also bring some security problems.

In addition, in practical applications, a large number of wireless transmission technologies are used, and most of the equipment is in an unattended state, making information security not guaranteed and easy to be stolen and maliciously tracked. The leakage of private information and malicious tracking have brought great security risks to users.

The

the source of the wisdom of the
IoT

applications "smart", such as smart transportation, smart logistics, smart buildings, etc., where the wisdom comes from this layer.
When a large amount of information generated by the sensing layer is transmitted through the network layer and gathered to the management layer, if it cannot be effectively integrated and utilized, it is no different from entering the mountain of treasure and returning, looking at the "ocean of data" and sighing.
The management layer solves the problems of
how to store data, how to retrieve it, how to use it, and how not to be abused

When we enjoy the life convenience brought by

, invisible security threats may have occurred.
Recently,
IoT
technology has continued to develop and innovate, profoundly changing the traditional industrial form and people’s lifestyle. As hundreds of millions of devices are connected to the
IoT
to provide innovative and interconnected new services, fraud and attacks in the entire ecosystem have increased, and the impact on user privacy and the security of the basic network environment is particularly prominent.

IoT Becomes Disaster Area of Information Security
One by one,
these are
becoming a
reality.
Time to secure
them is now.
IoT
has gradually become the “harder-hit area” of network security, but currently
IoT
hardware equipment manufacturers have weak security awareness and insufficient security investment, such as video surveillance equipment, traffic sensing equipment.
There have been many cases of home cameras being controlled by hackers and using illegally obtained sensitive videos to blackmail users.
In August 2017, the police in a place of Zhejiang uncovered a criminal gang that produced and distributed home camera cracking software on the Internet.
Approximately 10,000 cracked and intruded home camera IPs were seized, involving Zhejiang, Yunnan, Jiangxi and other provinces.
At the "2018
isc
(Internet Security Conference)", many experts believed that the huge base of
IoT
devices, coupled with fragile security protection, it is foreseeable that
IoT
threats will gradually become the norm on the Internet.

One by one,
these are
becoming a
reality.

them is now.
Consumer
IoT
privacy leaks frequently:
consumer
IoT
is based on
comsumption
, a network of smart devices created for the purpose of using
IoT
smart devices to greatly improve or influence people's consumption habits.
Smart homes, smart wearable devices such as bracelets, glasses, and portable medical devices are the main applications of consumer
IoT
. The consumer
IoT
scenario is closest to a large number of terminal sellers, so it has also received more attention from the black industry chain.
The portable insulin pump launched by a British medical company was remotely controlled by hackers. Hackers can completely control the injection metering, which directly affects the life safety of users.
In 2017, there were a number of ransomware incidents targeting smart TVs in Japan.

IoT Becomes Disaster Area of Information Security
One by one,
these are
becoming a
reality.
Time to secure
them is now.
For many ordinary people, security threats may be lurking around. Once an attacker obtains remote control permissions, even a small camera can become the culprit for leaking user privacy.
The latest Gartner survey data found that nearly 20% of organizations have suffered at least one
IoT
-based attack in the past three years.
In order to guard against these threats, Gartner predicts that global
IoT
security spending will reach 1.5 billion dollars in 2018, an increase of 28% from 2017's 1.2 billion dollars. It is expected that
IoT
security spending will reach 3.1 billion dollars in 2021.

Attack surface continues to expand:
It is estimated that the number of global IoT connections will exceed 30 billion by 2026. The massive number of devices connected to the network has formed an unprecedentedly large attack surface, and any weak node may become a breakthrough point.
Staggering economic losses:
Cybersecurity Ventures predicts that the global economic losses caused by IoT attacks will reach $2.1 trillion by 2025, covering multiple costs such as data breaches, business interruptions and device repairs.
Edge devices become a disaster zone:
The Verizon DBIR 2025 report shows that the frequency of exploit targeting network edge devices has surged nearly 8 times compared to previous years, and the security protection of edge computing environments is facing severe challenges.
IoT Becomes Disaster Area of Information Security

IoT Becomes Disaster Area of Information Security
One by one,
these are
becoming a
reality.

them is now.
As a new technology, the industry standards and related management of the Internet of Things have just started, but the
IoT
has a large base, rapid spread, and low technical threshold, which has become a security issue that has to be paid attention to on the Internet.
Wu
Yunkun
, president of 360 Enterprise Security Group, said that the main threats to the consumer
IoT
are as follows:
First, use vulnerabilities or covert behaviors such as automatic software installation to steal user privacy such as files and videos;
Second, spread bots to turn smart devices into tools for hijacked use;
Third, hackers can use the control equipment to reversely attack the enterprise internal or its running cloud platform to steal or destroy data.

Case 1: RCtea Botnet (Early 2026)
Scale and Targets: It successfully infected nearly 10,000 IoT devices in just 5 days, mainly targeting unprotected edge devices such as home and commercial cameras, and routers. The attack purpose is clear: it uses controlled "bots" to launch Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks, causing severe congestion to the target network services.
Case 2: Joint Attack by Aisuru and Kimwolf (2025-2026)
Super Scale and Destruction: A huge botnet composed of 1 to 4 million infected devices set a new record with a DDoS attack traffic peak of over 30 Tbps. The attack was finally detected and dismantled with the assistance of cybersecurity companies such as Akamai, exposing the grim reality that IoT devices have become hackers' "arsenals".

Security Characteristics of IoT
One by one,
these are
becoming a
reality.
Time to secure
them is now.
(1) Some existing security solutions for sensor networks, the Internet, mobile networks, secure multi-party computing, cloud computing, etc. can be partially used in the IoT environment, but other parts may no longer be applicable
First, the number of sensor networks corresponding to the IoT and the scale of terminal objects are much larger than those of a single sensor network
Second, the processing capabilities of the terminal equipment or devices connected to the IoT will be very different, and they need to interact with each other
Third, the amount of data processed by the IoT will be much larger than the current Internet and mobile networks

(2)  Even if the security of the perception control layer, the data transmission layer, and the intelligent processing layer are separately guaranteed, the security of the
IoT
cannot be guaranteed.
This is because the
IoT
is a large-scale system integrating several layers, and many security problems stem from system integration;
The data sharing of the
IoT
puts forward higher requirements for security;
The application of the
IoT
will put forward new requirements for security. For example, privacy protection is not a single-level security requirement, but it is an indispensable security requirement for
IoT
application systems.
In view of the above reasons, the development of the
IoT
needs to re-plan and formulate a sustainable development security architecture, so that the security protection measures of the
IoT
can be continuously improved during the development and application of the
IoT
.

Regardless of the diversification of the sources and channels of security threats and the generalization of sources, we can summarize the security requirements of the
IoT
into the following aspects:
IoT
access security,
IoT

IoT

and other aspects.
IoT
access security
In access security, the access security of the sensing layer is the key point. First, a sensing node cannot be accessed by a node or system that has not been authenticated and authorized, which involves the security requirements of the sensing node's trust management, identity authentication, and access control. Therefore, in addition to being subject to the same security threats as existing networks, sensor networks may also be subject to security threats such as attacks from malicious nodes, monitoring or destruction of transmitted data, and poor data consistency.

Security Demands of IoT
One by one,
these are
becoming a
reality.
Time to secure
them is now.
IoT
communication security
Due to the exponential growth of communication terminals in the
IoT
and the limited carrying capacity of existing communication networks, when a large number of network terminal nodes access the existing network, it will bring more security threats to the communication network.
First, the access of a large number of terminal nodes will definitely bring about network congestion, and network congestion will give attackers an opportunity to take advantage of, thereby causing a denial of service attack on the server;
Second, due to the small amount of data transmitted by devices in the
IoT
, complex encryption algorithms are generally not used to protect data, which may cause data to be attacked and destroyed during transmission;
Finally, the integration of the sensing layer and the network layer will also bring some security problems.
In addition, in practical applications, a large number of wireless transmission technologies are used, and most of the equipment is in an unattended state, making information security not guaranteed and easy to be stolen and maliciously tracked. The leakage of private information and malicious tracking have brought great security risks to users.

Security Demands of IoT
One by one,
these are

reality.
Time to secure
them is now.
IoT
data privacy security
With the development and popularization of the
IoT
, data has exploded. Individuals and companies are pursuing higher computing performance, and software and hardware maintenance costs are increasing, making the equipment of individuals and companies no longer able to meet their needs. Therefore, cloud computing, grid computing, pervasive computing, cloud computing, etc. have emerged. Although these new computing models solve the equipment needs of individuals and businesses, they also risk losing direct control over their data.
Therefore, the security and privacy protection technology for outsourced data in data processing is particularly important. Since traditional encryption algorithms perform poorly in the calculation and retrieval of
ciphertexts
, it is very necessary to study encryption algorithms that can be retrieved and operated in the
ciphertext
state.

IoT
computing system security
The application field of the
IoT
is very wide, and it has penetrated into all walks of life in real life. Due to the particularity of the
IoT
itself, its application security problems exist in addition to the common security threats in existing network applications, and there are more special problems of application security.
In
IoT
applications, in addition to the security requirements of traditional networks (such as authentication, authorization, auditing, etc.), it also includes the privacy and security requirements and service quality requirements of
IoT
application data, and the security requirements of application deployment.

The
IoT
can build different security architectures from different perspectives. With reference to the system architecture of the
IoT
, we can divide the information security system of the
IoT
into a four-layer system architecture:
IoT
sensing security,
IoT
transmission security,
IoT
data security, and
IoT
application security.
At each layer, a variety of information security technologies must be used, such as identity authentication, access control, data encryption, digital signatures, and security protocols.
